title: Green Energy Projects Face New Delays in Europe and the UK
subtitle: From car manufacturing rules to steel production, major green projects are struggling to meet deadlines.

summary.en: Car manufacturers are asking the European Commission to delay new trade rules for electric vehicles. The industry says it cannot meet targets for making batteries locally by 2027. Meanwhile, Tata Steel faces a significant delay for its new electric furnace in Wales. These problems highlight the difficulty of moving to greener technology. Both governments and companies are now looking for ways to solve these infrastructure and supply chain issues.
summary.zh: 汽車製造商正要求歐盟執委會延後針對電動車的新貿易規則。產業界表示，無法達成二〇二七年前在當地製造電池的目標。與此同時，塔塔鋼鐵在威爾斯的新型電爐計畫面臨嚴重延宕。這些問題凸顯了轉型至更環保技術的困難。政府與企業目前正尋求解決這些基礎設施與供應鏈問題的方法。

paragraphs.en: Major green energy projects in the UK and Europe are facing new challenges that threaten to slow down the transition to cleaner technology. From the production of electric vehicles (EVs) to the modernization of steel plants, companies are struggling to meet strict deadlines. These delays are causing concern among industry leaders who worry that official targets are becoming impossible to reach.
paragraphs.zh: 英國與歐洲的大型綠能計畫正面臨新的挑戰，這些挑戰恐將拖慢轉型至更潔淨技術的腳步。從電動車的生產到鋼鐵廠的現代化，企業正苦於達成嚴格的期限。這些延宕引起了產業領袖的擔憂，他們擔心官方設定的目標將變得遙不可及。

paragraphs.en: In the car industry, manufacturers are currently pressing the European Commission to change the rules of the Brexit trade deal. They are asking for a second delay to new tariffs—taxes on imported goods—that are set to begin on January 1, 2027. Under the current agreement, cars sold without these taxes must meet strict rules about where their parts are made. Specifically, 55% of a car’s value, along with a large percentage of its battery components, must be produced within Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 在汽車產業方面，製造商目前正敦促歐盟執委會（European Commission）修改英國脫歐貿易協定的規則。他們要求針對預計於二〇二七年一月一日生效的新關稅（即進口商品稅）進行第二次延期。根據現行協議，若要免除這些關稅，汽車必須符合關於零件產地的嚴格規定。具體而言，汽車價值的百分之五十五，以及絕大部分的電池組件，都必須在歐洲境內生產。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, the industry originally hoped these rules would encourage local battery manufacturing. However, global events like the COVID-19 pandemic and the war in Ukraine caused major shortages of parts. As a result, the goal of making batteries in Europe has been much harder than expected. Jonathan O’Riordan, an official at the European Automobile Manufacturers’ Association (ACEA), noted that while the industry once hoped for 60% of batteries to be made in Europe by 2027, the current estimate is now just under 20%.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，產業界原本希望這些規則能鼓勵在地電池製造。然而，新冠肺炎疫情與烏克蘭戰爭等全球事件，導致零件嚴重短缺。結果，在歐洲製造電池的目標變得比預期困難許多。歐洲汽車製造商協會（ACEA）官員奧里奧丹（Jonathan O’Riordan）指出，儘管產業界曾希望到二〇二七年時，有百分之六十的電池能在歐洲製造，但目前的預估值已降至不到百分之二十。

paragraphs.en: Industry leaders argue that the supply chain for batteries is simply not ready. Mike Hawes, chief executive of the UK’s Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders (SMMT), explained that the current requirements were based on assumptions that have not come true. He warned that the UK and the EU must find a practical solution to avoid adding costs to the very vehicles that consumers are being encouraged to buy. Sigrid de Vries, the director general of ACEA, added that the development of battery technology in Europe is moving too slowly and requires a shift in government policy to speed up the process.
paragraphs.zh: 產業領袖主張，電池供應鏈根本尚未準備就緒。英國汽車製造商與貿易商協會（SMMT）執行長霍斯（Mike Hawes）解釋說，目前的規定是基於一些尚未實現的假設。他警告，英國與歐盟必須找到務實的解決方案，以免增加消費者被鼓勵購買的車輛成本。歐洲汽車製造商協會總幹事德弗里斯（Sigrid de Vries）補充說，歐洲電池技術的發展進度過慢，需要政府政策轉向以加速此一進程。

paragraphs.en: One of the biggest problems is the high cost of production. Making batteries in Europe remains about 30% more expensive than in China. Furthermore, China currently controls the supply of important raw materials like lithium. Building a full production chain for these materials is a slow and expensive process, with some factory projects costing hundreds of millions of dollars.
paragraphs.zh: 其中最大的問題之一是高昂的生產成本。在歐洲製造電池的成本仍比中國高出約百分之三十。此外，中國目前掌控了鋰等重要原料的供應。建立一套完整的原料生產鏈是一個緩慢且昂貴的過程，部分工廠計畫的成本高達數億美元。

paragraphs.en: While the car industry deals with trade rules, the steel industry is facing its own set of infrastructure problems. BBC Business reports that Tata Steel may face a delay of up to eight months for its new £1.25 billion electric furnace in Port Talbot. This project is a key part of the company’s plan to replace older, more polluting blast furnaces. However, the company has warned that it is having trouble securing the high-power electricity needed to run the new facility.
paragraphs.zh: 當汽車產業忙於應對貿易規則時，鋼鐵產業也面臨自身的基礎設施問題。英國廣播公司（BBC）商業新聞報導，塔塔鋼鐵（Tata Steel）位於塔爾伯特港（Port Talbot）價值十二點五億英鎊的新型電爐計畫，可能會面臨長達八個月的延宕。該計畫是公司汰換舊型高污染高爐計畫的核心部分，但公司已發出警告，表示在取得運作新設施所需的高功率電力方面遭遇困難。

paragraphs.en: Koushik Chatterjee, an executive at Tata Steel, explained that the project is currently in a difficult position. The company is working with the National Grid—the organization responsible for the UK’s electricity infrastructure—to upgrade the power supply. However, the National Grid has reported its own delays in completing the necessary electrical work, which includes building new substations and laying underground cables. These infrastructure challenges are critical because the new furnace cannot operate without a reliable, high-power connection.
paragraphs.zh: 塔塔鋼鐵高階主管查特吉（Koushik Chatterjee）解釋說，該計畫目前處於艱難處境。公司正與負責英國電力基礎設施的國家電網公司（National Grid）合作，以升級電力供應。然而，國家電網公司回報其在完成必要的電力工程（包括興建新變電站與鋪設地下電纜）方面也出現延誤。這些基礎設施挑戰至關重要，因為若沒有可靠的高功率連接，新電爐將無法運作。

paragraphs.en: Tata Steel is currently in discussions with the UK government and its partners to see if they can find a way to speed up the project. The company noted that timelines for such large construction projects often change as engineering work progresses. Despite these setbacks, the company remains focused on delivering the project safely.
paragraphs.zh: 塔塔鋼鐵目前正與英國政府及其合作夥伴商討，尋求加速計畫的方法。公司指出，這類大型建設工程的時程表往往會隨著工程進展而變動。儘管遭遇這些挫折，公司仍致力於安全地完成該計畫。

paragraphs.en: These two situations show the complex nature of moving toward a greener economy. Whether it is the need for more local battery production or the requirement for massive upgrades to the electrical grid, the transition requires more than just good intentions. As both the car and steel industries have shown, success depends on having the right infrastructure, enough raw materials, and policies that match the reality of the current market. For now, both governments and private companies are under pressure to find solutions that keep these important projects moving forward without causing further economic harm.
paragraphs.zh: 這兩種情況顯示了邁向綠色經濟的複雜本質。無論是需要更多的在地電池生產，還是對電網進行大規模升級，轉型所需的遠不止良好的意圖。正如汽車與鋼鐵產業所展現的，成功取決於是否具備完善的基礎設施、充足的原料，以及符合當前市場現實的政策。目前，政府與民間企業皆面臨壓力，必須尋求解決方案，在不造成進一步經濟損害的前提下，讓這些重要計畫持續推進。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To strongly urge or demand that someone takes a specific action.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞（緊急的），這裡作動詞用，表示向某人施壓要求行動。文中：In the car industry, manufacturers are currently pressing the European Commission to change the rules of the Brexit trade deal.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To happen in the way that was expected or predicted.
keyTerms.noteZh: 雖然常用於「夢想成真」，但在這裡指預測或假設變成了現實。文中：Mike Hawes, chief executive of the UK’s Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders (SMMT), explained that the current requirements were based on assumptions that have not come true.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A group of similar things that belong together.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（放置），這裡作名詞，指一系列相關的問題。文中：While the car industry deals with trade rules, the steel industry is facing its own set of infrastructure problems.

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: Car industry pressing EU for further delay to Brexit EV tariffs

sources.name: BBC Business
sources.title: Tata Steel says new £1.25bn furnace may be delayed due to electrical issue

previousArticles.title: Challenges Grow for Green Energy Projects in the UK and Europe
previousArticles.summaryEn: The car industry is asking the European Commission to delay new rules on electric vehicle trade. Manufacturers argue they cannot meet strict local production targets for batteries by 2027. Meanwhile, Tata Steel faces a potential eight-month delay for a major new electric furnace project in Wales. These setbacks highlight the difficulty of building the necessary infrastructure for a greener economy. Both sectors are now calling for urgent government support to keep their environmental goals on track.

previousArticles.title: The UK's Electric Vehicle Debate: A New Challenge for Green Energy
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United Kingdom is facing a difficult debate over its electric vehicle goals. New data shows that recent changes to government rules could lead to 17 million extra tonnes of carbon dioxide. Car makers argue that current targets are too difficult to reach in the current market. Meanwhile, charging companies warn that changing the rules again will hurt their investments. The government plans to review these policies again by early 2027.

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the current industry estimate for the percentage of battery components that will be produced in Europe by 2027?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，Jonathan O’Riordan 指出雖然業界曾希望達到 60% 的目標，但目前的預估數字已降至 20% 以下。選項 A 的 55% 是現行貿易規則要求的比例，而非目前的生產預估值。選項 C 的 60% 是產業最初的期望值，而非現況。選項 D 的 30% 是歐洲相較於中國的生產成本差距，與電池生產佔比無關。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the relationship between the car industry's goals and the current trade deal rules?

options.A: The industry believes the current rules will make electric vehicles cheaper for consumers.
options.B: The rules were designed based on a set of global conditions that have since changed significantly.
options.C: The car manufacturers are confident they can meet the 2027 deadline without any changes.
options.D: The trade deal was specifically created to prevent the use of Chinese lithium in European cars.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段，Mike Hawes 指出目前的規定是基於一些「未實現的假設」（assumptions that have not come true），結合第三段提到的疫情與戰爭影響，可推論出當初制定規則時的全球環境與現今大不相同。選項 A 錯誤，因為業界擔心規則會增加消費者購買成本。選項 C 與文章提到的「尋求延期」矛盾。選項 D 在文中未提及，文章僅提到中國控制鋰礦供應，未說明貿易協定是為了禁止其使用。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'intentions' most likely refer to in the context of the green transition?

options.A: The official plans and goals set by governments and industries.
options.B: The personal feelings of the factory workers.
options.C: The legal threats made by trade organizations.
options.D: The financial investments made by private banks.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到「the transition requires more than just good intentions」，意指單靠良好的願景或目標是不夠的，必須要有實際的基礎建設與政策支持。選項 B 與文中討論的工業轉型層面無關。選項 C 的法律威脅並非此處「intentions」的含義。選項 D 雖然與資金有關，但「intentions」在此處明確是指政策制定者與企業對綠色轉型的期望或目標，而非具體的投資行為。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the transition to a greener economy?

options.A: The transition is being successfully completed ahead of schedule in the UK and Europe.
options.B: The steel industry is finding it much easier to transition than the car industry.
options.C: The shift to green energy is primarily a financial problem that can be solved by printing more money.
options.D: Achieving green energy goals is a complex process that requires more than just ambition; it requires infrastructure and realistic policies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。最後一段總結了文章核心，強調綠色轉型面臨複雜挑戰，不僅需要良好的意圖，更需要基礎建設、原物料供應與務實的政策配合。選項 A 與文章開頭提到的「面臨挑戰」與「進度緩慢」完全相反。選項 B 文中並未比較兩者誰較容易，僅指出兩者皆面臨困難。選項 C 將問題過度簡化為金錢問題，忽略了文中強調的基礎建設與供應鏈等結構性因素。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
